advantages: De facto standard in Python scientific computing—examples, tutorials, and library docs assume Matplotlib syntax.
Fine-grained control over every artist: colors, markers, ticks, annotations, and multi-panel layouts.
Works headless in scripts and CI via the Agg backend; same code runs in notebooks and batch jobs.
Foundation layer for Seaborn, pandas plotting, and many ML metric visualizations—skills transfer upward.
disadvantages: Verbose compared to high-level libraries—simple statistical plots require more boilerplate than Seaborn.
Default styling can look dated without rcParams or style sheets; publication polish takes extra steps.
Large scatter plots of millions of points can be slow without downsampling, datashader, or alternative renderers.
pyplot global state is easy to misuse in functions, producing subtle bugs with multiple figures.
